QF.id: XVI Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_60

question.text: Commodities stored in exchange-accredited warehouses for the purpose of clearing and settlement must meet:

answer.text: Quality and quantity parameters as specified in the contract specifications
Only weight requirements — quality is not important
Standards set by individual warehouse operators
No specific standards — any commodity is accepted

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Stored commodities must meet both quality AND quantity parameters as per contract specifications. Assayers certify quality, and warehouses maintain storage standards. **The Trap:** Both quality AND quantity matter. Exchange contract specs (not warehouse operators) set the standards. Standards absolutely exist. **Cross-Exam Context:** Quality assurance in commodity settlement is unique to this market. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Goods not meeting quality specs will be rejected for delivery — sellers must get quality certification from exchange-approved assayers before depositing goods in the warehouse.
